message.content: Adversaries may exploit the COR_PROFILER environment variable to hijack the execution flow of .NET CLR programs, leveraging it to load malicious DLLs. This technique allows attackers to establish persistence, elevate privileges, and impair defenses by executing a malicious DLL in the context of all .NET processes whenever the CLR is invoked. The COR_PROFILER can be set at system, user, or process scopes, with system and user-wide settings specified in the Registry, and process scope settings created in-memory. Starting with .NET Framework 4, the profiling DLL does not need to be registered if its location is specified in the COR_PROFILER_PATH environment variable. Adversaries, such as Blue Mockingbird, have used file and Registry modifications to set the COR_PROFILER variable, ensuring the execution of malicious DLLs. Mitigation strategies include monitoring and restricting changes to environment variables and the Registry, employing application whitelisting, and using endpoint detection and response (EDR) tools to detect and block suspicious DLL loading activities.
